mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS against 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in fewer than 55% of observation months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending expectations, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of months where UNRATE increases by over 0.3 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand growth, which drives corporate revenue acceleration and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive growth signal in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production indicates immediate supply-side contraction that forces firms to cut output and guidance, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during economic slowdown phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines do not precede negative 5-day forward returns during identified slowdown periods, or the signal shows positive predictive power instead.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of imminent monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Months with CPIAUCSL increases above 0.4 percent yield positive 20-day forward returns in more than 58% of cases, invalidating the tightening fear channel.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic capacity and rising household income, which supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows a negative or statistically insignificant correlation with 60-day forward returns across a 5-year rolling evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising housing starts and falling retail sales indicates capital shifting toward construction rather than consumer goods, creating sector rotation pressure that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The HOUST-RSAFS divergence signal fails to predict negative 20-day forward returns with a hit rate above 50% in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous spike in industrial production and CPI inflation signals an overheating supply bottleneck that forces immediate margin compression, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 1.0% and CPIAUCSL rises > 0.3% in 1 month
failure_condition: The joint INDPRO-CPIAUCSL threshold condition yields positive 5-day forward returns in more than 65% of triggered instances, refuting the margin compression mechanism.
